title: Electrical engineers
description: Research, design, develop, test, or supervise the manufacturing and installation of electrical equipment, components, or systems for commercial, industrial, military, or scientific use. Research, design, develop, or test electronic components and systems for commercial, industrial, military, or scientific use employing knowledge of electronic theory and materials properties. Design electronic circuits and components for use in fields such as telecommunications, aerospace guidance and propulsion control, acoustics, or instruments and controls. Design and implement radio frequency identification device (RFID) systems used to track shipments or goods.

tasks.task: Confer with engineers, customers, or others to discuss existing or potential engineering projects or products.
Compile data and write reports regarding existing or potential electrical engineering studies or projects.
Inspect completed installations and observe operations to ensure compliance with specifications and safety standards.
Estimate labor, material, or construction costs for budget preparation purposes.
Integrate electrical systems with renewable energy systems to improve overall efficiency.
Design, implement, maintain, or improve electrical instruments, equipment, facilities, components, or systems for various applications.
Conduct field surveys or study data to identify and correct power system problems.
Develop software to control electrical systems or electronic components.
Assist in developing capital project programs for new equipment or major repairs.
Oversee project production efforts to ensure projects are completed on time and within budget.
Operate computer-assisted engineering or design software or equipment to perform engineering or electronics tasks.
Direct or coordinate activities related to the manufacture, construction, installation, maintenance, or operation of electronic systems.
Prepare technical drawings, specifications of electrical systems, or documentation for projects to ensure compliance with standards.
Create simulations or models of radio frequency identification device (RFID) systems and conduct tests for system performance.
Train users in system operations and provide technical support for systems, including radio frequency identification device (RFID) technology.
Analyze electronic system requirements and project feasibility based on capacity, cost, or customer needs.
Investigate or test vendors' or competitors' products to inform engineering decisions.
Prepare budget or cost estimates for equipment, construction, or installation projects.
Perform systems analysis or programming for radio frequency identification device (RFID) technology including testing.
Identify operational requirements for new systems and determine the usefulness of new technologies for project integration.

detailed_work_activities.title: Estimate technical or resource requirements for development or production projects.
Test products for functionality or quality.
Confer with technical personnel to prepare designs or operational plans.
Discuss designs or plans with clients.
Train personnel on proper operational procedures.
Test performance of electrical, electronic, mechanical, or integrated systems or equipment.
Analyze design requirements for computer or electronics systems.
Design electronic or computer equipment or instrumentation.
Investigate system, equipment, or product failures.
Monitor the productivity or efficiency of industrial operations.
Survey land or bodies of water to measure or determine features.
Develop software or applications for scientific or technical use.
Design control systems for mechanical or other equipment.
Supervise engineering or other technical personnel.
Direct equipment maintenance or repair activities.
Research design or application of green technologies.
Advise customers on the use of products or services.
Inspect finished products to locate flaws.
Conduct validation tests of equipment or processes.
Evaluate characteristics of equipment or systems.
Analyze operational data to evaluate operations, processes or products.
Install instrumentation or electronic equipment or systems.
Communicate technical information to suppliers, contractors, or regulatory agencies.
Explain project details to the general public.
Provide technical guidance to other personnel.
Recommend technical design or process changes to improve efficiency, quality, or performance.
